title: Integer square root: isqrt n satisfies r^2 <= n < (r+1)^2
statement_informal: The integer square root isqrt n is the unique r with r^2 <= n < (r+1)^2. isqrt_aux scans the candidate upward while (r+1)^2 <= n; with fuel S n it reaches the boundary. isqrt_correct: isqrt n * isqrt n <= n /\ n < S (isqrt n) * S (isqrt n).

cost_model.notes: Upward scan; the lower bound r^2<=n is an invariant (only step to S r when (S r)^2<=n), the upper bound n<(S r)^2 holds when the scan stops by the condition (reached with fuel S n).

references: Integer square root (floor of the real square root); a building block for primality/factoring (e.g. trial division up to isqrt n).
